reasoning: The Lean formalization uses `∃! p : ℕ × ℕ` with `p.1 > 0 ∧ p.2 > 0` correctly capturing "unique positive integers $a, n$". The equation `p.1^(p.2+1) - (p.1+1)^p.2 = 2001` encodes $a^{n+1} - (a+1)^n = 2001$. Although ℕ subtraction is truncated, this does not change the solution set: if $a^{n+1} \geq (a+1)^n$ the ℕ subtraction agrees with integer subtraction, and if $a^{n+1} < (a+1)^n$ it yields 0 ≠ 2001, so no spurious solutions are introduced and none are lost. The intended solution $(13, 2)$ (where $13^3 - 14^2 = 2001$) satisfies both formulations identically. The Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
